design_philosophy.core_loop: Create virtual people (Sims), place them in houses, direct their moods, satisfy their desires, and observe emergent life simulation
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Managing competing Sim needs (hunger, hygiene, social, career) against limited time and resources
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Playing god as a benevolent architect of suburban life; building homes and directing the intimate daily routines of virtual people
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Sandbox-first design with no mandatory goals or win conditions; intentionally satirizes U.S. consumer culture through materialism mechanics; inspired by Maslow's hierarchy of needs and urban design philosophy rather than traditional game mechanics

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: No failure states or difficulty settings documented; players can modify gameplay freely as a sandbox with no punishment system

progression.upgrade_system: Sims can improve skills, advance careers, and purchase home furnishings and objects to increase property value and Sim comfort
progression.unlock_mechanism: Career advancement unlocks new job levels; expansion packs augmented what players could do with their Sims
replayability_hooks: Procedural Sim personality generation creates unique behavioral combinations
Open-ended sandbox allows infinite playstyle variations
Seven expansion packs extend gameplay with new objects, locations, and mechanics
Pre-constructed homes and building tools enable creative expression

world_building_techniques: Suburban setting near SimCity establishes a living world
Career tracks provide implicit narrative progression
Sim personality traits and aspirations (in later titles) create emergent storytelling
Home decoration and object placement allows player-authored narratives

faction_indicators: Career paths (business, entertainment, culinary, etc.) signal social roles
Aspiration system in The Sims 2 creates distinct personality factions
danger_signals: Low hygiene leads to illness and reduced effectiveness
Neglected social needs result in loneliness and depression
Failed career performance leads to job loss
House fires and accidents from poor maintenance
environmental_storytelling: Home decoration choices reveal player priorities and Sim identity
Lot placement and customization reflects taste and resources
Object hoarding or minimalism signals behavioral patterns
